_publications._publicationTitle: Assessment of RAS Dependency for BRAF Alterations Using Cancer Genomic Databases.

_contextSnippet: both BRAF class 1 alterations and fusions are mutually exclusive to coexisting RAS alterations (P < .001 by χ2 for both; odds ratios range from 0.003-0.13 and 003-0.73, respectively)
details: Successfully analyzed coalteration patterns showing class 1 BRAF alterations and fusions are mutually exclusive to RAS alterations (P < 0.001, odds ratios 0.003-0.13 and 0.003-0.73 respectively)
